news.headline: Global web hosting services market to grow by USD 124.2 billion (2024-2028) at a CAGR of 16.8%, driven by e-commerce and AI transformation.
news.summary: A recent report exploring the influence of artificial intelligence on market trends has revealed significant findings. The global web hosting services market is projected to experience substantial growth, with an estimated increase of USD 124.2 billion expected between 2024 and 2028, as indicated by Technavio. This growth translates to a projected compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 16.8% throughout the forecast period.

news.headline: Capital Power is doing a $350 million Common Shares Bought Offering; Base Shelf Prospectus available, prospectus supplement in two days on SEDAR+.
news.summary: The base shelf prospectus is now available for public access, and the prospectus supplement will be accessible on SEDAR+ within the next two business days. Stay tuned for further details.

news.headline: GameStop's Q3 revenue of $860.3M missed estimates, cash stands at $4B+, no offerings planned this fiscal year.
news.summary: GameStop Corp (NYSE:GME) recently released its third-quarter financial results after the market closed on Tuesday. The company reported total net sales of $860.3 million for the quarter, a decrease from last year's third-quarter total of $1.08 billion. This fell short of the Street consensus estimate of $887.7 million. Sales were broken down by segment as follows, with last year's third-quarter totals provided in parentheses: Hardware and accessories at $417.4 million ($579.4 million), software at $271.8 million ($321.3 million), and collectibles at $171.1 million ($177.6 million). All three segments experienced declines in sales compared to the previous year.

news.headline: Manulife re-appoints Ernst & Young LLP as external auditor. Board endorses decision.
news.summary: Manulife Financial Corporation, listed under TSX/NYSE/PSE as MFC and SEHK as 945, disclosed on Dec. 10, 2024, that its Board of Directors has approved the re-hiring of Ernst & Young LLP ("EY") as their auditor.

news.headline: US Ambassador to UK Hartley discusses 2025 Geopolitics.
Chainlink partners with Coinbase to enhance Project Diamond with secure data transmission and interoperability.
news.summary: Chainlink has been chosen to facilitate the transmission of verifiable data and crosschain interoperability for Coinbase's Project Diamond. With this collaboration, Chainlink will provide the necessary infrastructure to ensure secure and reliable data transmission. This partnership is set to enhance Coinbase's capabilities in processing and utilizing various forms of data. The move is also expected to boost Coinbase's overall efficiency and competitiveness in the market.

news.headline: Sportsman's Warehouse Holdings, Inc. reported a net loss of $0.4 million for the third quarter of fiscal year 2024, with net sales at $324.3 million, a decrease of 4.8% compared to the same period in 2023. Adjusted EBITDA was $16.4 million. For the thirty-nine weeks ended November 2, 2024, net sales were $857.2 million, a decrease of 6.6%, with a net loss of $24.3 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $15.1 million. The company adjusted its guidance for fiscal year 2024, expecting net sales in the $1.18 billion to $1.20 billion range and adjusted EBITDA to be between $23 million to $29 million. The company also expects total inventory to be below $350 million. CFO highlighted additional marketing pressures on margins and strategic inventory investments to improve sales during the season. Company plans to reduce inventory levels to generate positive free cash flow. Management remains focused on improving topline with margin and profitability in the mid and long term. Adjusted diluted earnings per share were $0.04 for the third quarter 2024, compared to adjusted diluted loss per share of $(0.01) in the same period of the prior year. Emphasis was placed on the balance sheet and ending the year with positive free cash flow. The financial results indicate ongoing efforts to navigate challenges in the current consumer environment while focusing on marketing initiatives and inventory management to drive sales.
news.summary: Sportsman's Warehouse Holdings, Inc. released its third-quarter financial results for the thirteen weeks ended November 2, 2024. Net sales were $324.3 million, a decrease of 4.8% compared to the same period in 2023. Same-store sales decreased by 5.7%, gross profit was $103.1 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $16.4 million. For the thirty-nine weeks ended November 2, 2024, net sales were $857.2 million, a decrease of 6.6% compared to the previous year. The company expects net sales and adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 to be in the range of $1.18 billion to $1.20 billion and $23 million to $29 million, respectively. The company plans to open one new store in fiscal year 2025, with total inventory expected to be below $350 million. The company emphasized traffic-driving marketing and product pricing initiatives to navigate the challenging consumer environment. Non-GAAP financial measures were included in the report, and the company addressed forward-looking statements and factors influencing future results. The company's net loss for the third quarter was $(0.4) million, compared to a net loss of $(1.3) million in the same quarter of 2023. Adjusted net income for the quarter was $1.4 million compared to an adjusted net loss of $(0.2) million in the third quarter of fiscal year 2023. Adjusted EBITDA was $16.4 million compared to $16.2 million in the third quarter of fiscal year 2023. The company ended the third quarter with net debt of $151.3 million and total liquidity of $150.8 million.

Each share has a par value of $0.01. The company, listed on NASDAQ as MDRR, aims to manage its outstanding shares effectively.

news.headline: Medicover AB (Publ) considering separate listing for Indian hospital subsidiary in India to boost growth in 12-24 months.
news.summary: The board of directors of Medicover AB (Publ) in Stockholm announced on December 10, 2024, their intention to assess the possibility of listing their Indian hospital subsidiary in India within the next 12-24 months. This move is aimed at bolstering the company's expansion efforts in the rapidly growing Indian market.

news.headline: Americans overwhelmingly oppose proposed Medicare cuts according to RPAN survey.Calls for Congress action.
news.summary: The Radiology Patient Action Network (RPAN) is urging Congress to take action before the end of the year. According to a recent national survey, a majority of Americans are against proposed Medicare reductions that would impact their healthcare. The findings highlight strong opposition to the cuts, emphasizing the importance of addressing this issue promptly.

news.headline: Eagle Materials to acquire Bullskin Stone & Lime, a Western PA aggregates business, for $152.5 million. Expected to close in 60 days.
news.summary: Dallas-based Eagle Materials Inc. (NYSE: EXP) has revealed its plans to acquire Bullskin Stone & Lime, LLC, an aggregates company in Western Pennsylvania, for a total of $152.5 million. Bullskin Stone & Lime, LLC focuses on serving the Pittsburgh area and the wider Western Pennsylvania region. The acquisition deal is set to be finalized in the upcoming two months, pending the fulfillment of standard closing requirements.

news.headline: Abcourt Mines Inc. reports 97%+ votes for all resolutions at the Annual Shareholders Meeting on Dec. 10, 2024.
news.summary: In Rouyn-Noranda, Quebec on December 10, 2024, Abcourt Mines Inc. ("Abcourt" or the "Corporation") (TSX Venture: ABI) (OTCQB: ABMBF) shared positive results from its Annual and Special Meeting of Shareholders. The Meeting saw the approval of all resolutions presented, with over 97% of shares voted in favor.

news.headline: American Strategic Investment Co. to sell its 9 Times Square property for $63.5 million; closing set for Dec 19, 2024. Buyer paid $7 million deposit.
news.summary: Today, American Strategic Investment Co. (NYSE: NYC) revealed that the sale of its 9 Times Square Midtown Manhattan property ("9 Times Square") is set to close on December 19, 2024. The contract sales price for the property is $63.5 million, with the buyer already submitting a $7 million non-refundable deposit. This transaction follows an amendment secured by the Company regarding the sale.

news.headline: Amgen declares $2.38 per share dividend for Q1 2025, to be paid on March 7, 2025.
news.summary: A company based in Thousand Oaks, California, made an announcement on December 10, 2024, regarding its quarterly dividend. Amgen (NASDAQ: AMGN) declared a dividend of $2.38 per share for the first quarter of 2025. This dividend is set to be paid on March 7, 2025, to all stockholders of record as of the end of business on a specified date.

news.headline: Dave & Buster's Q3 2024 financials: $453.0M revenue (down 3%), net loss $32.7M, adjusted EBITDA $68.3M (down 16.3%). CEO transition announced.
news.summary: Dave & Buster's Entertainment, Inc. reported financial results for Q3 2024 with revenue at $453.0 million, down 3.0% from Q3 2023. Comparable store sales decreased by 7.7%. Net loss was $32.7 million compared to $5.2 million in Q3 2023. The company also reported an adjusted EBITDA of $68.3 million, showing a decrease of 16.3% from the previous year. The CEO has resigned, and the board is looking for a replacement. The company opened three new stores during the quarter, refinanced debt worth $700.0 million, and raised $28.5 million through a sale leaseback transaction. They have repurchased $88.0 million in shares year-to-date. Despite challenges faced, the management remains positive about the strategic direction of the company. The company ended the quarter with $8.6 million in cash and $537.4 million available under its revolving credit facility. The net total leverage ratio was 2.6x, and $112.0 million remains for share repurchases. They also released their Form 10-Q and will host an investor conference call. Overall, the company is focused on growth and enhancing shareholder value.

news.headline: Dave & Buster's (NASDAQ: PLAY) disappoints in Q3 earnings, missing estimates by -25% with EPS of $-0.45.
news.summary: Dave & Buster's Enter, listed on NASDAQ as PLAY, released its third-quarter earnings report on December 10, 2024, at 04:15 PM. The company fell short of projected earnings by -25%, with an earnings per share (EPS) of -$0.45. Investors can find more details on this update through Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Arogo Capital Acquisition Corp. signs LOI with Bangkok Tellink for potential business combo, making the latter a US publicly traded company.
news.summary: On December 10, 2024, Arogo Capital Acquisition Corp. (OTC: AOGO) from Miami, Florida, revealed a significant development. The company has entered into a binding letter of intent (LOI) with Bangkok Tellink Co., Ltd., based in Bangkok. Bangkok Tellink is recognized for its expertise in advanced telecommunications, mobile network technology, and Internet of Things (IoT) solutions. This agreement outlines the initial terms for a potential merger, which could lead to Bangkok Tellink becoming a publicly traded entity in the United States.

news.headline: Aizip and SoftBank launch custom Small Language Models for enterprise apps to ensure data safety; 100x accuracy.
news.summary: In Cupertino, California, Aizip, Inc. collaborating with SoftBank Corp., unveiled specialized Small Language Model (SLM) and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) solutions tailored for enterprise use. This technology can function on mobile devices or on on-premises servers, focusing on enhancing enterprise data security. These SLMs are optimized with domain-specific data to handle distinct enterprise operations and boast comparable accuracy to cloud LLMs that are 100 times larger.

news.headline: VICI Properties Inc. amends lease with PURE Canadian Gaming, now acquired by Indigenous Gaming Partners Inc. ($NYSE: VICI)
news.summary: In New York, VICI Properties Inc. (NYSE: VICI) revealed an agreement where they allowed the assignment of the master lease agreement with PURE Canadian Gaming Corp. to an affiliate of Indigenous Gaming Partners Inc. This move is tied to the acquisition of PURE's operating assets by the IGP affiliate. IGP, comprised of five institutional First Nations, was formed to engage in gaming activities. VICI Properties continues to showcase strategic involvement in these transactions reflecting their active role in the gaming sector.

news.headline: ACELYRIN, INC. reports Phase 2b/3 trial results for Izokibep in treating Uveitis, not meeting primary or secondary endpoints.
news.summary: A recent phase 2b/3 trial of izokibep did not meet its primary endpoint. Additionally, the secondary endpoints of the study also did not show statistical significance. This trial involved approximately [number] participants and aimed to assess the efficacy of izokibep for a specific condition. However, the results indicate that the drug did not demonstrate the desired effectiveness in meeting the predetermined primary and secondary goals of the trial. Further analysis and discussions may be necessary to determine the next steps for this investigational drug.

news.headline: Dow Jones lags, AI stock rises for third day, Bitcoin surpasses $100,000; market updates in real-time.
news.summary: The Dow Jones index underperformed compared to other major indexes. Bitcoin saw a price increase exceeding $100,000 on Friday. Moreover, AI stock experienced a notable surge for the third consecutive day, while Bitcoin stocks also rallied.

news.headline: MAA (NYSE: MAA) increases quarterly dividend payment to $1.515 per share, payable on Jan 31, 2025.
news.summary: Mid-America Apartment Communities, Inc. in Germantown, Tennessee, disclosed on December 10, 2024, that its board of directors sanctioned a quarterly dividend of $1.515 per common stock share. This payment is scheduled for January 31, 2025. Shareholders as of that date will be eligible to receive the dividend.

news.headline: Chimerix grants 385,000 stock options to 3 new employees under Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4) on Dec 2, 2024.
news.summary: In Durham, NC, on Dec. 06, 2024, Chimerix disclosed that inducement awards were given by the Compensation Committee of its Board of Directors on December 2, 2024, to three new employees. These awards consist of non-statutory stock options enabling the purchase of a combined 385,000 shares of Chimerix’s common stock. The approval for these awards was granted by the Compensation Committee as an inducement crucial to the new employees’ employment, following Nasdaq Listing rule 5635(c)(4).

news.headline: Cellectis draws final €5 million tranche from €40 million credit facility with EIB for CAR T-cell products.
news.summary: On December 10, 2024, Cellectis (Euronext Growth: ALCLS – NASDAQ: CLLS) announced the final €5 million tranche drawdown under a credit facility agreement with the European Investment Bank, totaling €40 million. This agreement was signed on December 28, 2022. The full amount is expected to be disbursed by December 18, 2024. The Company intends to allocate the funds towards the development of its allogeneic CAR T-cell product candidates, including UCART22 and UCART20x22.

news.headline: Travel + Leisure Co. secures $875 million term loan, repricing and refinancing transaction, replaces $593M and refinances $282M loans.
news.summary: Travel + Leisure Co. (NYSE:TNL) has finalized the Sixth Amendment to its Credit Agreement, bringing forth an $875 million incremental term loan referred to as the “2024 Term Loan B Facility.” This new facility is set to mature on December 14, 2029, with pricing at SOFR plus 250 basis points at par. The 2024 Term Loan B Facility effectively replaces and reprices the previous $593 million borrowed under the 2023 incremental term loan B facility, in addition to refinancing the $282 million outstanding.

news.headline: MAA appoints A. Bradley Hill (48) as CEO effective April 1, 2025, replacing H. Eric Bolton, Jr. (68) who is retiring.
news.summary: In Germantown, Tennessee on December 10, 2024, Mid-America Apartment Communities, Inc., "MAA" (NYSE: MAA), revealed A. Bradley Hill (48) will take over as President and CEO from April 1, 2025, following the retirement of H. Eric Bolton, Jr. (68).

news.headline: GM will cease robotaxi operations previously supported by CEO Barra.
Notation Labs secured $2 million credit facility to boost production of Qwel™ for lead detection, smart home tech.
news.summary: A U.S.-based facility in Scottsdale, Arizona received funding totaling $2 million to enhance production capabilities and introduce cutting-edge lead detection and smart home technology to the market. Notation Labs Inc. confirmed the conclusion of the credit facility on December 10, 2024, signaling a significant step towards expanding operations and innovation in the industry.

news.headline: Argentina’s Milei dismisses tax agency head after imposing tax on streamers, appoints new leader.
news.summary: The country's President, Javier Milei, has recently made changes to the leadership of the revenue agency. This decision comes after the implementation of a new tax system aimed at digital creators. The revamped leadership is expected to oversee the enforcement and administration of this taxation. The move signifies a shift in the government's approach towards regulating the digital economy and ensuring compliance. The exact number of officials replaced has not been disclosed, but it is a significant development in the country's fiscal policies under Milei's administration.

news.headline: Neal Gerber Eisenberg to move to Chicago landmark The Bell in 2025, occupying floors 27-29, from current office.
news.summary: In a recent announcement, Neal Gerber Eisenberg (NGE) revealed their intention to relocate to The Bell at 225 West Randolph Street, Chicago, in May 2025. This move marks a shift from their present location at Two North LaSalle Street. The newly renovated site, formerly known as the Illinois Bell Building and recognized as a Chicago landmark in 2021, holds historical significance since its establishment in 1966. Upon relocation, NGE will be situated on floors 27 to 29 of the iconic tower. The transition promises modern office spaces for attorneys and staff to operate efficiently.

news.headline: Luke Johnson Scholarship in Scottsdale offers $1,000 one-time award to support entrepreneurial innovators.
news.summary: In Scottsdale, Arizona on December 10, 2024, the Luke Johnson Scholarship for Entrepreneurs has revealed its mission to aid upcoming entrepreneurial innovators with a one-time award of $1,000. Luke Johnson, a respected entrepreneur from Scottsdale, established this scholarship with the purpose of supporting the future generation of business pioneers.

news.headline: Artisan Partners Asset Management Inc. reported $166.8 billion AUM on November 30, 2024, with Artisan Funds and Artisan Global Funds totaling $80.5 billion, and separate accounts accounting for $86.3 billion. Artisan Funds made distributions with $314 million not reinvested.
news.summary: Artisan Partners Asset Management Inc. announced that its preliminary assets under management as of November 30, 2024, amounted to $166.8 billion. Among this total, Artisan Funds and Artisan Global Funds represented $80.5 billion while separate accounts and other assets under management accounted for $86.3 billion. During November, specific Artisan Funds distributed their annual income and capital gains. The month-end AUM for November reflects the influence of roughly $314 million from Artisan Funds distributions that were not reinvested.

news.headline: IFF names Michael DeVeau as CFO effective Jan. 1, 2025, succeeding Glenn Richter who will retire by end of 2024.
news.summary: In New York, IFF (NYSE: IFF) has appointed Michael DeVeau as its new Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, starting on Jan. 1, 2025. DeVeau, who currently serves as Senior Vice President of Corporate Finance and Investor Relations, will take over from Glenn Richter, the current CFO and Business Transformation Officer, upon his planned retirement at the end of 2024. The company expressed their satisfaction with DeVeau's promotion, describing him as the ideal candidate for the role.

news.headline: AEO Inc. (NYSE: AEO) declares $0.125 per share dividend, payable on Jan 24, 2025, to stockholders of record on Jan 10, 2025.

This dividend will be paid on January 24, 2025, to stockholders of record as of January 10, 2025. AEO is a prominent global specialty retailer with popular apparel brands like American Eagle, Aerie, and OFFL/NE by Aerie.

The Andersons, Inc. appoints Weston Heide and Mark Simmons as EVPs to lead Agribusiness and Renewables segments. Effective Jan 1, 2025.

They will head the Agribusiness and Renewables segments starting January 1, 2025. This move marks a transition to a two-segment system within the company.
